Class PerlBuilder

java.lang.Object
io.perl.api.impl.PerlBuilder
public final class PerlBuilder extends Object
Helper class responsible for constructing configured PerL instances and their supporting components (time implementations, latency windows, and periodic recorders) based on supplied configuration and logger choices.

  • Constructor Summary

    Build Latency Record Window using configured strategy (array or hashmap) depending on memory estimation and latency range.

  • Method Details

    • buildTime

      @NotNull public static @NotNull Time buildTime(@NotNull @NotNull PerformanceLogger logger)
      Build 'Time' object based on Performance logger. The returned Time implementation matches the logger's TimeUnit.
      Parameters:
      logger - Performance logger
      Returns:
      Time object appropriate for the logger's time unit

    • buildLatencyRecordWindow

      @NotNull public static @NotNull LatencyRecordWindow buildLatencyRecordWindow(@NotNull @NotNull LatencyConfig config, Time time, long minLatency, long maxLatency, double[] percentileFractions)
      Build Latency Record Window using configured strategy (array or hashmap) depending on memory estimation and latency range.
      Parameters:
      config - Latency configuration
      time - Time instance used for conversions
      minLatency - Minimum Latency value (inclusive)
      maxLatency - Maximum Latency value (inclusive)
      percentileFractions - Array of percentile fractions (e.g. {0.5,0.9})
      Returns:
      a configured LatencyRecordWindow instance

    • build

      @Contract("null, _, _, _ -> fail; !null, null, _ -> fail") @NotNull public static @NotNull Perl build(PerformanceLogger logger, Time time, PerlConfig config, ExecutorService executor) throws IllegalArgumentException, IOException
      Build CQ (Concurrent Queue) based Perl.
      Parameters:
      logger - Performance Logger
      time - time interface
      config - Perl configuration
      executor - Executor Service
      Returns:
      Perl Object
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - in case logger and latency reporter are missing and time unit of 'Time' and performance logger is not matching
      IOException - if the CQ perl creation failed.
